title: code me pinescript LuxAlgo Price Action Concepts

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: code me pinescript LuxAlgo Price Action Concepts

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
I am unable to directly provide a Pinescript code since I am an AI Language model, and it is critical to understand that I can only help you with guidance.

However, I can guide you to proceed coding in Pinescript's LuxAlgo and some Price Action concepts.

I assume you already have a basic understanding of the Pinescript language, and you want to use LuxAlgo Indicator, which is a TradingView indicator. LuxAlgo is a premium indicator, which means you need to acquire access to it to use it in your Pinescript code.

Once you have access to the LuxAlgo indicator, you can follow these steps:

1. Begin by defining inputs for LuxAlgo, such as LuxAlgo's length, signal factor, and sensitivity.

```pinescript
//@version=4
study("LuxAlgo + Price Action", shorttitle="LA+PA", overlay=true)

// Inputs for LuxAlgo
len = input(14, "Length", type=input.integer)
sf = input(7, "Signal Factor", type=input.integer)
sens = input(40, "Sensitivity", type=input.integer)
```

2. Add LuxAlgo Indicator

```pinescript
luxAlgo = ta.atr("+2")
[indicator, signal] = request.security(syminfo.tickerid, "D", luxAlgo(len, sf, sens))
plot(indicator, color=color.blue)
plot(signal, color=color.orange)
```

3. Code Price Action Concepts

Price action refers to the evaluation of price movements without using indicators.

3.1. Support and Resistance Zones

```pinescript
ssResMultiplier = input(1.2, "Support & Resistance Multiplier", type=input.float)
lookBack = input(50, "Bars for Support & Resistance")

suppResPivot(sr) =>
    srLow = lowest(low, sr)
    srHigh = highest(high, sr) * ssResMultiplier
    srLow == srHigh ? srLow : na

support = suppResPivot(lookBack)
resistance = suppResPivot(lookBack)

plot(support, "Support", color=color.green)
plot(resistance, "Resistance", color=color.red)
```

3.2. Pin Bars

```pinescript
pinbarLen = floor(len / 2)

isPinBarBearish() =>
    high == highest(high, pinbarLen) and close < open

isPinBarBullish() =>
    low == lowest(low, pinbarLen) and close > open

bgcolor(isPinBarBearish() ? color.red : na, transp=85)
bgcolor(isPinBarBullish() ? color.green : na, transp=85)
```

Feel free to modify or get inspirations from the code above to suit your own strategy. Note that the provided example is meant to illustrate some possible concepts and might not yield the desired results; proper analysis, backtesting, and optimization are necessary.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Since you are looking for a premium version of the LuxAlgo indicator with Price Action in Pine Script for TradingView, I would like to remind you that I cannot provide copyrighted code or material that belongs to someone else.

However, I can still help you with integrating the LuxAlgo Premium Indicator and some price action concepts in a Pine Script code. To access the LuxAlgo Premium Indicator, you must purchase it or have authorized access. After that, you can combine the premium indicator with the following code.

I'm assuming you already have a LuxAlgo Premium Indicator added to your collection. You can use the following Pine Script code as a template to combine the LuxAlgo Premium Indicator with some price action concepts:

```pinescript
//@version=4
study("LuxAlgo + Price Action (Premium)", shorttitle="LA+PA", overlay=true)

// Inputs for LuxAlgo (use settings based on the premium version)
len = input(14, "Length", type=input.integer)
sf = input(7, "Signal Factor", type=input.integer)
sens = input(40, "Sensitivity", type=input.integer)

// Add LuxAlgo Indicator
luxAlgo = ta.atr("+2")
[indicator, signal] = request.security(syminfo.tickerid, "D", luxAlgo(len, sf, sens))
plot(indicator, color=color.blue)
plot(signal, color=color.orange)

// Price Action Concepts
// Support and Resistance Zones
ssResMultiplier = input(1.2, "Support & Resistance Multiplier", type=input.float)
lookBack = input(50, "Bars for Support & Resistance")

suppResPivot(sr) =>
    srLow = lowest(low, sr)
    srHigh = highest(high, sr) * ssResMultiplier
    srLow == srHigh ? srLow : na

support = suppResPivot(lookBack)
resistance = suppResPivot(lookBack)

plot(support, "Support", color=color.green)
plot(resistance, "Resistance", color=color.red)

// Pin Bars
pinbarLen = floor(len / 2)

isPinBarBearish() =>
    high == highest(high, pinbarLen) and close < open

isPinBarBullish() =>
    low == lowest(low, pinbarLen) and close > open

bgcolor(isPinBarBearish() ? color.red : na, transp=85)
bgcolor(isPinBarBullish() ? color.green : na, transp=85)
```

The given example is intended to demonstrate some possible concepts, such as support and resistance zones and pin bars, for price action. The specific values and concepts used in the example may not necessarily yield the desired results. It is essential to analyze, backtest, and optimize the strategy accordingly.

Please make sure to replace the LuxAlgo function with the appropriate settings and functions from the LuxAlgo Premium Indicator, as I cannot provide details for copyrighted material.
